learning_outcomes.description: Describe the drivers of sustainable practices in contemporary business and key concepts in sustainability including principles of governance, corporate legitimacy, stakeholder engagement and disclosure.

Explain key aspects of strategy, planning and reporting in sustainable business practice, including the Global Reporting Initiative, the CERES Roadmap, various relevant standards and certification schemes and how to apply the concept of Materiality.

Apply and demonstrate an understanding of how to estimate, monitor and manage inputs and outputs like water, energy, waste and emissions from organisations, products and services, particularly in the built environment.
Distinguish features and principles of sustainable procurement, lean supply chain management and sustainable design

Relate how human rights and social justice principles feature in sustainable business practice, particularly in regards to securing licenses to operate, effective employee management and ethical engagement with stakeholders, notably vulnerable groups and indigenous people.
Critique the influence of the banking, insurance and investment sector on sustainable business practice expectations and standards.

assessments.task_description:
Purpose: Analyze ESG reporting practices of a chosen company using recognized frameworks (e.g., GRI, SASB, TCFD).

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Online submission by Turnitin only by the due date and time. No hard copy or assignment cover sheets are required. Submission via email is not accepted.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
Project Report: 9/10/2026 2:00 pm

Group Presentations will take place in the Tutorials in Week 11-12.
assessments.task_description:
Components:

  • Group Project Report (15%)
  • Group Presentation (5%)

A group of students (at most 3) will do a 10-minute in-class group presentation. Develop and present an ESG strategy for a company or sector incorporating circular economy, stakeholder engagement, and environmental governance.

Working in teams, students develop a sustainability transformation strategy for a company, industry sector, or organisation.

Examples:

  • Net Zero Transition Plan
  • Circular Economy Roadmap
  • Sustainable Supply Chain Strategy
  • Climate Adaptation Strategy
  • Biodiversity Action Plan
  • ESG Improvement Plan

Students must:

  1. Identify key sustainability challenges
  2. Conduct stakeholder analysis
  3. Evaluate trade-offs
  4. Propose strategic interventions
  5. Develop implementation pathways

The report will be submitted online via Turnitin through Blackboard, with one group member submitting on behalf of the group.

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
This assessment is based on students' quality and consistency of participation throughout the semester. Students will be assessed on their contributions and active participation in weekly tutorials through:

  • ESG case study discussions
  • Sustainability reporting critiques
  • Stakeholder mapping exercises
  • Circular economy business simulations
  • Climate-risk scenario analyses
  • Sustainable finance debates
  • Group problem-solving activities
  • Peer feedback exercises
assessments.submission_guidelines: There is no student submission. Tutors will record participation progressively using a marking rubric across the semester.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: As this relates to participation across the semester, and there is no student submission, late penalties are not applicable.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
The examination may include a combination of:

• Multiple-choice questions

• Short-answer questions

• Scenario-based questions

• Applied problem-solving questions

• Short essay questions

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.
